behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real STT model diarization quality; a stubbed transcript with perfect labels would hide missed/incorrect speaker tags. Two-pass (above-Integration): INTENT TAG=model-output -> Agentic. Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets one model output property (speaker labels), not a chained multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the production STT API with diarization enabled, then assert transcript segments include stable speaker labels and that labels are preserved into the summary input payload.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic parsing/mapping logic that can fail within one local function/object boundary. No real OS/network/subprocess or model judgment is required to expose extraction bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures with varied assignment phrasings and assert exact extracted tuples (assignee, task text) in the intermediate summary-input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on LLM content decisions (coverage/completeness per attendee). Mocked LLM output would always satisfy fixture expectations and miss real omissions. Two-pass: INTENT TAG=model-output -> Agentic. Rejected Workflow because the behavior checks one summary-output quality criterion, not a required multi-agent chain assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript inputs with known assignees and call the real summarization model; assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one action item in the structured summary.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff/schema-wiring behavior: extractor output must be correctly consumed by summary-schema assembler. Unit tests on one function can miss contract mismatches between modules. Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no dependence on real model output if decisions are pre-identified fixtures; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real local extraction + schema-mapping modules with fixture transcripts/identified-decision objects, then assert decisions populate only the `decisions` field and are absent from generic narrative slots.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This validates semantic extraction/placement quality of real model output. Deterministic stubs hide failures where the model misses or misplaces decisions. Two-pass: INTENT TAG=model-output -> Agentic. Rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on one output quality property, not multi-step agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Send real transcript samples containing explicit decisions to the production summarizer and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and not only in free-text narrative.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This is a multi-step agentic pipeline: segment -> repeated STT/LLM processing -> merge coherence across steps. Failures emerge from sequencing and cross-step context loss, not one isolated call. Two-pass: INTENT TAG=multi-step-agentic -> Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation and sequential processing, then validate all segments were processed in order and final merged summary remains coherent and structurally complete across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Explicit end-to-end pipeline with multiple agentic stages (STT + LLM summarization) and real handoffs. Real failures are in chained execution, retries/state propagation, and final delivery. Two-pass: INTENT TAG=multi-step-agentic -> Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production pipeline on a real meeting recording from upload through final delivery and assert successful completion, valid structured schema, and no manual recovery steps.
